claim: The parity sequence of Fibonacci numbers begins 0,1,1,0,1,1.
justification: Compute recurrence modulo 2 from F_0=0 and F_1=1.

claim: The parity pattern repeats with period 3.
justification: The ordered pair (F_n,F_{n+1}) modulo 2 returns to (0,1) after three recurrence updates.

claim: F_n is even exactly when n is congruent to 0 modulo 3.
justification: The zero terms occur at the first position of each repeated parity cycle.

claim: Every finite DAG has at least one source vertex.
justification: If every vertex had an incoming edge, finite backward traversal would produce a directed cycle.

claim: Repeatedly removing a source vertex yields an ordering.
justification: The removal preserves acyclicity and terminates by finiteness.
